---
title: "Welcome to On Guard"
date: "2026-04-02"
categories: [meta, guardians]
description: "Introducing On Guard — a baseball data science blog built on Statcast graphs."
---

## What is this?

On Guard is a baseball data science blog built on a Neo4j graph of Statcast data.
The goal is to explore pitch and swing mechanics, player similarity, and situational
tendencies — with a particular focus on the Cleveland Guardians.

## The stack

Analysis is driven by a graph where every pitch is a node connected to:

- The **pitcher** who threw it
- The **batter** who faced it
- Its **pitch mechanics cluster** (velocity, movement, spin)
- Its **swing mechanics cluster** (bat speed, attack angle, swing length)
- The **zone** it landed in
- The **contact outcome** if it was put in play

Node embeddings are generated via FastRP in Neo4j GDS, capturing each player's
structural fingerprint across the season. Predictive models use XGBoost on top of
those embeddings in a staged pipeline.

## Coming up

- Swing archetype breakdown for the 2025 Guardians roster
- Pitch cluster analysis — what makes José Ramírez hard to pitch to
- Batter similarity: who swings like whom

Stay tuned.
